posture.label: Barbell
posture.explanation: Base case: AI boom and monetary stress coexist; keep AIM-5 balance.

ai_productivity.interpretation: Revenue growth, margins, free cash flow margin, R&D intensity, and optional SEC filing-language monetization evidence. Leaders: META, NVDA, MSFT.

ai_bubble_risk.interpretation: Capex growth, capex/revenue, debt growth, depreciation growth, free-cash-flow pressure, and optional SEC filing-language risk evidence. Leaders: GOOGL, AMZN, AMD.

monetary_reset.interpretation: One or more weighted FRED macro/credit inputs are older than 90 days as of 2026-06-30 (World Credit Growth, U.S. Credit Growth, Federal Debt Growth, Federal Interest Payments Growth; oldest 272 days); score is directional only.

energy_bottleneck.interpretation: EIA retail electricity data: commercial electricity price +4.8% YoY; commercial electricity sales +5.2% YoY; industrial electricity price +5.5% YoY. First-pass proxy for AI/data-center energy bottleneck pressure.

hard_money_repricing.interpretation: Local BTC and PAXG gold-proxy prices are available; hard-money repricing score includes 200-week MA (primary), power-law gap (secondary), BTC/gold ratio, and gold proxy.

aim5_allocation.role: Semiconductors, AI platform, and test infrastructure bottlenecks.
Toll collectors every agent needs: payments, trust, proprietary data.
Power generation and grid bottleneck behind AI electrification.
Gold hedge. BTC lives outside per v0.4 doctrine.
Rare-earth optionality for electrification and semiconductor supply.
T-bills for drawdown ammunition and deployment triggers.

signals.name: AI Productivity Score
signals.direction: higher_is_better
signals.note: Revenue growth, margins, free cash flow margin, R&D intensity, and optional SEC filing-language monetization evidence. Leaders: META, NVDA, MSFT.

signals.name: AI Capex Bubble Risk
signals.direction: higher_is_riskier
signals.note: Capex growth, capex/revenue, debt growth, depreciation growth, free-cash-flow pressure, and optional SEC filing-language risk evidence. Leaders: GOOGL, AMZN, AMD.

signals.name: Energy Bottleneck Score
signals.direction: higher means power-cost/load pressure is rising
signals.note: EIA retail electricity data: commercial electricity price +4.8% YoY; commercial electricity sales +5.2% YoY; industrial electricity price +5.5% YoY. First-pass proxy for AI/data-center energy bottleneck pressure.
signals.value_label: commercial electricity price +4.8% YoY; commercial electricity sales +5.2% YoY; industrial electricity price +5.5% YoY

signals.name: BTC 200-Week Moving Average
signals.direction: below 200wMA = oversold (bullish for hard money); above = normal
signals.note: BTC spot is $58,667 versus a 200-week MA of $62,243, a -5.7% deviation. Model-free long-term trend signal.

signals.name: BTC Power Law Fair Value Gap
signals.direction: higher means BTC is repricing above the time-based power-law anchor
signals.note: BTC spot is $58,667 versus a power-law anchor of $137,034, a -57.2% gap. Gap has been below -40% for 38 consecutive days.

signals.name: BTC/Gold Ratio
signals.direction: higher means BTC is repricing versus the gold proxy
signals.note: BTC buys roughly 14.6 ounces of the PAXG gold proxy.

signals.name: Gold Proxy Price
signals.direction: higher means the gold shield is repricing in USD terms
signals.note: PAXG gold proxy is $4,007.

signals.name: U.S. M2 Liquidity Context
signals.direction: domestic liquidity context; not the core world-credit thesis signal
signals.note: US M2 is +6.3% YoY at $23,063B; useful context, but world credit is the monetary reset anchor.

signals.name: Fed Balance Sheet YoY
signals.direction: higher adds liquidity; sustained runoff keeps pressure on credit
signals.note: Fed assets are +0.8% YoY at $6,736B.

signals.name: Reverse Repo Buffer
signals.direction: lower means less residual liquidity buffer
signals.note: Overnight reverse repo is $27B, a thin remaining buffer.

signals.name: Treasury General Account
signals.direction: higher can drain private liquidity
signals.note: TGA is $919B, which keeps liquidity conditions tighter.

signals.name: Net Liquidity Proxy
signals.direction: higher supports risk appetite; lower supports stress
signals.note: Simple WALCL minus RRP minus TGA proxy is $5,790B; roughly 90d change is +0.1%.

signals.name: World Credit Growth
signals.direction: higher means the global stock of paper claims is expanding
signals.note: Total reporting countries credit to the non-financial sector is +5.7% 3Y annualized at 262,666.1; this is the core monetary reset signal, with U.S. M2 demoted to context.
signals.staleness_warning: FRED observation is 272 days old as of 2026-06-30; treat this score as directional only until the series refreshes.

signals.name: U.S. Credit Growth
signals.direction: higher can signal domestic credit expansion and monetary stress
signals.note: U.S. total credit to the non-financial sector is +6.0% YoY at 77,279.7.
signals.staleness_warning: FRED observation is 272 days old as of 2026-06-30; treat this score as directional only until the series refreshes.

signals.name: Federal Debt Growth
signals.direction: higher means fiscal debt load is compounding faster
signals.note: Federal debt is +7.9% YoY at 39,065,421.0.
signals.staleness_warning: FRED observation is 180 days old as of 2026-06-30; treat this score as directional only until the series refreshes.

signals.name: Federal Interest Payments Growth
signals.direction: higher means fiscal interest expense pressure is rising
signals.note: Federal government interest payments is +6.5% YoY at 1,218.9.
signals.staleness_warning: FRED observation is 180 days old as of 2026-06-30; treat this score as directional only until the series refreshes.

signals.name: 10Y Treasury Yield
signals.direction: higher yields tighten discount rates and debt-service conditions
signals.note: 10Y Treasury yield is 4.38%.

signals.name: 10Y Real Yield
signals.direction: higher real yields increase monetary and credit pressure
signals.note: 10Y real yield is 2.16%.

signals.name: 10Y Breakeven Inflation
signals.direction: higher breakevens can signal inflation repricing pressure
signals.note: 10Y breakeven inflation is 2.24%.

signals.name: High-Yield Option-Adjusted Spread
signals.direction: higher spreads mean credit stress is rising
signals.note: High-yield option-adjusted spread is 2.80 percentage points.

debate_question: Is AI producing real productivity faster than credit/fiscal stress is degrading money?
